hero_subtitle: SFTY connects KYC and KYB provider outputs to create auditable, risk-based onboarding workflows that support thorough review and evidence preservation for high-risk customers.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Onboarding slows when risk is unclear
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to efficiently review high-risk customers due to disconnected KYC and KYB data from multiple providers. This fragmentation creates delays, manual reconciliation, and weak audit trails that complicate defensible onboarding decisions.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates KYC, KYB, sanctions and adverse media results into one risk-based onboarding workflow that links provider data, review status and audit-ready evidence for high-risk customer decisions.
workflow_title: From onboarding checks to approval decisions
workflow_description: SFTY connects KYC and KYB provider results with sanctions screening and risk-based routing to create reviewable cases leading to documented approval decisions.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond KYC checks. Structured onboarding control
why_sfty_description: SFTY turns fragmented KYC and KYB outputs into connected workflows, cases and evidence, enabling clearer, auditable decisions for high-risk customer onboarding reviews.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs managing high-risk customer reviews by connecting provider outputs into auditable workflows with evidence preservation and enterprise governance controls.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support high-risk customer onboarding reviews
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how onboarding workflows manage risk-based review steps
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how onboarding evidence supports audit-ready compliance cases
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how SFTY connects KYC and KYB provider outputs
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for high-risk onboarding?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a compliance layer that consolidates KYC, KYB, sanctions, PEP screening, and risk reviews into auditable workflows for high-risk customer onboarding in digital asset businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY structure onboarding workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures onboarding by integrating provider outputs, applying internal policies, routing risk-based reviews, creating cases, and maintaining decision logs for controlled and consistent compliance workflows.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve onboarding evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit-ready onboarding evidence by maintaining case history, decision logs, provider results, and regulatory mapping, supporting traceability for internal review or regulatory inspection.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY connect multiple KYC and KYB providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects multiple KYC and KYB providers, allowing clients to select and combine providers by jurisdiction, client type, or risk level within one compliance operating layer.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC and KYB providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace KYC or KYB providers but integrates their outputs into structured workflows, decisions, evidence and reporting within a connected compliance platform.
final_title: Structure high-risk onboarding into controlled workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY connects KYC, KYB, sanctions and screening outputs into risk-based onboarding cases with audit-ready evidence.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
